destinations.evidence: Payload derives scheduled obligations from parent Scheduler state, filters predicates, and synthesizes scheduled txs even when no pool tx exists.
destinations.snippet: compute_obligations(&mut scheduled_db, current_block, base_fee, None) ... filter_obligations_by_predicates(...) ... create_scheduled_execution_transaction_from_state(...)

destinations.evidence: compute_obligations applies bounded due-window/state/fee/executed constraints and caps obligations.
Pool-sourced scheduled transactions are fully verified before inclusion.

rationale: The packet shows the transition to explicit full C1-C10 filtering and shared contract-state obligation generation.
statement: Before scheduled transactions reach the payload scheduled execution loop, block building must evaluate them against Scheduler and RitualWallet parent-state constraints equivalent to C1-C10, admit only Valid results, and in current bounded context derive obligations from contract or synced scheduler state so due scheduled work can be included even when the pool lacks a transaction.
suggested_falsification_tests: Due Scheduler obligation absent from pool should still be synthesized.
C1-C10 failures should be filtered before execution.
summary: Scheduled inclusion is filtered through shared parent-state constraints in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Due Scheduler obligation absent from pool should still be synthesized; C1-C10 failures should not reach execution.
title: Scheduled inclusion is filtered by shared contract-state constraints
